headline: Mandatory sentencing affects 23 offences under Malaysian law
context: Legal reform advocates highlighted that 23 offences in Malaysian statutes still carry mandatory minimum sentences, limiting judicial discretion.

cards.big: 23 Malaysian offences still impose mandatory minimum sentences on judges
cards.sub: Judicial discretion restricted despite 2023 death penalty reforms

cards.big: Firearms Act 1960 Section 8 mandates death or life imprisonment
cards.sub: No room for judges to consider mitigating circumstances in sentencing

cards.big: Bar Council called for review of all 23 mandatory sentences in 2024
cards.sub: Proportionality in punishment is a recognised human rights principle

cards.big: Over 4,600 prisoners serving mandatory terms as of December 2024
cards.sub: Many are first-time offenders who received no sentencing mitigation

cards.big: Does mandatory sentencing deliver justice or merely uniformity?
Law Reform Commission recommended reducing mandatory sentences to 15 offences in 2022
